headline: Most common shot sequence: 'dink → dink' (1494 occurrences)
insight: Top sequences across 2845 transitions: ['dink→dink(1494)', 'drive→drive(503)', 'drive→dink(257)', 'dink→drive(225)', 'volley→volley(71)']. Pattern detection from video is novel but shot-by-shot reproducibility is uncertain (shot count agreement: 40%).

pricing.reasoning: Sportradar play-by-play = $500K+/yr for major sports. At 1/100th for pickleball: $5K-$20K/year for real-time shot sequence data.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Shot detection has only 40% reproducibility. Sequences are derived from individual shot detections, so errors compound. Treat as indicative, not statistically rigorous.

headline: Kitchen play yields 1.8% point win rate — but from highlight clips only
insight: Of 2089 kitchen shots detected: 1.8% won the point. Caveat: these are highlight clips (selected for exciting play), so kitchen effectiveness in general play may differ. Shot detection consistency: 40%.

pricing.reasoning: Hudl coaching tier: $200-2000/month. Kitchen analytics add-on: $100-$500/month.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Highlight clips select for dramatic moments. Kitchen win rates in ordinary play could be very different. Also: shot position detection (kitchen vs baseline) has not been separately verified.

headline: AI-estimated DUPR range: '3.5-4.0' is modal (169/191 clips)
insight: METHODOLOGY: Gemini estimates DUPR based on visual cues (shot speed, positioning, decision quality). Distribution: {'3.5-4.0': 169, '3.0-3.5': 13, '4.0-4.5': 3, '4.5-5.0': 1, '2.5-3.0': 1}. CRITICAL: Verification showed 0% agreement on DUPR estimates across re-analysis. This is NOT a reliable predictor in its current form.

pricing.reasoning: Novel product — no direct comparable. If validated against real DUPR data, could command $1K-$5K/month from tournament platforms. Currently: proof-of-concept only.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: 0% DUPR agreement in verification testing. The model defaults to '3.5-4.0' regardless of actual skill — this is likely prompt bias, not real prediction. Needs ground-truth DUPR data for calibration.
